5195	
5196	\leanok
5197	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with the linear dual (H2$'$)]
5198	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_dualequiv}
5199	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv}
5200	  \uses{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
5201	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M\) be an
5202	  \(S\)-module. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the formation of the
5203	  linear dual: the canonical map
5204	  \[
5205	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \to_S S\bigr)
5206	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
5207	    \bigl(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e M \to_R R\bigr),
5208	    \qquad \varphi \;\longmapsto\; e^{-1} \circ \varphi,
5209	  \]
5210	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence, with inverse \(\psi \mapsto e \circ \psi\). On both
5211	  sides the \(R\)-module structure is the \(S\)-module structure of \(M\) pulled back
5212	  along \(e\), so that \(r \cdot m = e(r) \cdot m\): the left-hand side is the
5213	  \(S\)-linear dual \((M \to_S S)\) regarded as an \(R\)-module by restriction, and the
5214	  right-hand side is the \(R\)-linear dual of the restricted module
5215	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e M\) into the restricted ground ring. This is the dual
5216	  analogue of \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}: it is to the internal hom
5217	  / dual exactly what that lemma is to the tensor product. It is the \emph{H2$'$
5218	  ingredient} of the restriction-compatibility isomorphism of the C-bridge
5219	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial} --- the ring-iso/dual commutation that, composed with
5220	  the open-immersion pushforward comparison H1, identifies the dual of a restriction
5221	  with the restriction of the dual.
5222	\end{lemma}
5223	
5224	\begin{proof}
5225	
5226	\leanok
5227	  The two assignments \(\varphi \mapsto e^{-1} \circ \varphi\) and
5228	  \(\psi \mapsto e \circ \psi\) are mutually inverse, since \(e \circ e^{-1} =
5229	  \mathrm{id}_S\) and \(e^{-1} \circ e = \mathrm{id}_R\) as ring homomorphisms, so the
5230	  two composites are the identity on functionals. Each assignment is \(R\)-linear:
5231	  additivity is immediate, and for a scalar \(r \in R\) the defining relation
5232	  \(r \cdot m = e(r) \cdot m\) of the pulled-back module structure makes each linearity
5233	  check a single rewrite --- \(e^{-1}\) (resp.\ \(e\)) intertwines the \(R\)-action on
5234	  the source with the \(R\)-action on the target precisely because \(e\) and \(e^{-1}\)
5235	  are inverse ring homomorphisms identifying \(R\) with \(S\). The forward map is
5236	  therefore an \(R\)-linear bijection, i.e.\ an \(R\)-linear equivalence; packaging the
5237	  two one-sided maps with the inverse identities gives
5238	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoDualEquiv}\).
5239	\end{proof}
5240	
5241	\begin{definition}
5242

5244	[Precomposition equivalence on dual sections]
5245	  \label{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv}
5246	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.dualPrecompEquiv}
5247	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual}
5248	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings and let \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\)
5249	  be an isomorphism of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. On a fixed open \(U\), precomposition
5250	  with \(e\) gives an \(R(U)\)-linear equivalence between the dual sections
5251	  \[
5252	    \mathtt{dualPrecompEquiv}\,e\,U \;\colon\;
5253	      \bigl(M'|_U \to R|_U\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \bigl(M|_U \to R|_U\bigr),
5254	      \qquad \varphi \;\longmapsto\; \varphi \circ e|_U,
5255	  \]
5256	  with inverse \(\psi \mapsto \psi \circ (e^{-1})|_U\). This is the section-level core of
5257	  the contravariant functoriality of the presheaf dual \(M \mapsto M^\vee\)
5258	  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual}) in isomorphisms: precomposing a functional on \(M'\) with
5259	  the comparison isomorphism produces a functional on \(M\), \(R(U)\)-linearly and
5260	  invertibly.
5261	\end{definition}
5262	
5263	\begin{definition}
5264	
5265	\leanok
5266	[The presheaf dual carries an iso \(M \cong M'\) to an iso \(M'^\vee \cong M^\vee\)]
5267	  \label{def:presheaf_dual_iso_of_iso}
5268	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso}
5269	  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv}
5270	  An isomorphism \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules induces a
5271	  canonical isomorphism of presheaf duals
5272	  \[
5273	    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e \;\colon\; M'^\vee \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; M^\vee
5274	  \]
5275	  --- note the reversal of direction, the dual being contravariant --- assembled
5276	  open-by-open from the precomposition equivalences
5277	  \(\mathtt{dualPrecompEquiv}\,e\,U\) of \cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv}.
5278	  Naturality in \(U\), i.e.\ compatibility of these sectionwise equivalences with
5279	  restriction, is the routine check that precomposition by \(e\) commutes with
5280	  restricting functionals.
5281	\end{definition}

5291	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(e : M \xrightarrow{\sim} M'\) an isomorphism in

5293	  duals
5294	  \[

5296	      \mathtt{dual}\,M,
5297	  \]
5298	  obtained by sheafifying the presheaf isomorphism \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e\) of
5299	  \cref{def:presheaf_dual_iso_of_iso} through the sheaf-level dual of
5300	  \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf}. It is the dual (contravariant) analogue of the
5301	  functoriality of the tensor product in isomorphisms: where an isomorphism of a tensor
5302	  factor induces an isomorphism of tensor products, an isomorphism \(M \cong M'\)
5303	  induces an isomorphism of duals in the reverse direction.
5304	\end{definition}

32	\section{Setup and statement}
33	\label{sec:thm32_setup}
34	
35	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), a smooth variety \(X\) over \(\bar k\) (in the
36	project's convention: a geometrically integral, geometrically reduced, separated scheme of
37	finite type over \(\bar k\) that is moreover smooth, hence nonsingular at every closed point),
38	and an abelian variety \(A\) over \(\bar k\) (in particular \(A\) is complete, smooth, and a
39	group object in \(\mathrm{Sch}/\bar k\)). A rational map
40	\(f \colon X \dashrightarrow A\) is, in the project's notation, the equivalence class of a
41	regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to A\) defined on a dense open subset
42	\(U \subseteq X\), two such pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\) and \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) being equivalent
43	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\).
44	
45	The result of this chapter is:
46	
47	\begin{theorem}
48
